note: STARTER holdout for the assess bake-off (#2555). Cases are difficulty-balanced (2 easy / 3 medium / 2 hard) and dimension-diverse, but their expected composite ranges are first-pass labels that need human review before this gate is trusted for real promotions. min_pass 7.0 is intentionally stricter than the rubric's day-to-day 5.5 floor — the sealed lock is the authoritative promotion bar. (2026-07-14 label review #2565: all 7 ranges verified defensible against their rubric dimensions — h1 clean/tested 8-10, h2 security-blocker 1-4, h3 N+1 perf 4-6.5, h4 no-authz money 3.5-6, h5 maintainability trade-off 4-6.5, h6 concurrency-race discriminator 3-6, h7 YAGNI over-engineering 4.5-7. No range changed → seal intact, no re-seal. Flag for owner: h4's upper bound 6.0 is slightly generous for a money endpoint with zero authorization — a strict assessor could argue security hits its blocker like h2; kept as a range pending the live bake-off. Remaining before this gate decides real promotions: one live-token bake-off (paid grader spend) — owner-consent-gated, not run autonomously.)
